title: memstead-plugin/v1 facet
description: A Facet is a named way a binding engages with a medium — a specific perspective from which the binding reads or writes it. Each facet file lives at `.memstead/facets/<mem>/<name>.json`; the filename stem is the facet's reference key, used by a binding's `source_facets`. A facet holds a selection (allow/deny `scope` over the referenced medium), an optional engagement contract (verbs/tools/discipline — free-form), and an optional deterministic preparation step. A facet always references exactly one medium. Unchanged from v0.

name.description: Stable reference name — bindings reference a facet by this. Conventionally equal to the filename stem.

medium.description: The medium (by name) this facet is a perspective on. Resolves to `.memstead/mediums/<mem>/<medium>.json` under the same mem directory.

scope.description: Allow/deny selection over the referenced medium. Empty (or absent) means the whole medium. Order matters: later entries override earlier ones.

engagement.description: Engagement contract — verbs, tools, terminology, discipline for operating on the medium in this perspective. Free-form: its shape differs by medium type and by source/destination side, and the plugin does not interpret it.

preparation.description: Optional deterministic preparation step (string identifier, e.g. `pdf-to-markdown`). Unset for every text medium today. A facet naming a preparation the engine has no implementation for is refused at binding-validation time — no silent skip, no crash.

path.description: Workspace-relative path or glob, interpreted relative to the referenced medium's pointer. Leading `../` is permitted. Glob semantics follow gitignore conventions; `**` crosses path separators, `*` does not.
